vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are either absent, dormant, or have suffered total systemic collapse.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_Yellow_Pot: Systemic Loss: No visible String of Nickels foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Black_Pot: Systemic Loss: No visible Mexican Mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Black_Pot: Systemic Loss: Pothos foliage is absent; only the white rabbit anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Black_Pot: Systemic Loss: No visible Silver Guest foliage.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil is bare.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, and potentially anaerobic. No fungal blooms detected, but surface texture suggests high moisture retention.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments are present in the central black pot, indicating a previous nutrient intervention or soil amendment attempt.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic debris outside the pots.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: The sequence shows a rapid decline. Early images show a singular green sprout/stem in the central pot, which progressively loses turgor and color, eventually disappearing or collapsing into the substrate.
temporal_deltas.Current_State: Total loss of visible vegetation across all monitored units.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total vegetative failure. The lack of photosynthetic material suggests the specimens have either been removed, decomposed, or died back completely into the soil.
anomalies: Total absence of expected plant life (Systemic Loss)
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical specimens. The chronological sequence documents the final stages of a failing biome, where the last remaining green stem (observed in T-5) gradually lost its structural integrity and vanished. The current state is characterized by bare, dark soil across all pots. The white rabbit anchor remains as the only constant in an otherwise depleted environment.
